<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request! Please fill in the sections below.
If anything doesn't apply, write "n/a" — don't delete the section, so reviewers know you considered it.
-->

## Summary

<!-- What does this PR do, and why? Keep it to a few lines. -->

## Related issues

<!-- Link issues this PR closes or relates to, e.g. "Closes #123". -->

## Type of change

<!-- Put an x in the boxes that apply. -->

- [ ] feat — new feature
- [ ] fix — bug fix
- [ ] docs — documentation only
- [ ] chore — tooling, CI, refactor (no behaviour change)
- [ ] test — tests only
- [ ] breaking change — introduces an incompatible API change

## How has this been tested?

<!--
Describe the tests you ran and how reviewers can reproduce them.
New features and bug fixes should come with tests that fail without the change.
-->

## Screenshots / output

<!-- Optional: screenshots, CLI output, or recordings for UI or user-visible changes. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
- [ ] My commits are [signed-off (DCO)](https://github.com/cognitx-leyton/.github/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#5-developer-certificate-of-origin-dco) (`git commit -s`)
- [ ] I targeted the correct base branch (`dev` if it exists, otherwise `main`)
- [ ] I have added or updated tests where appropriate
- [ ] I have updated documentation where the change affects usage
- [ ] I have run the linter/formatter and type checker locally
- [ ] CI is green on this PR
- [ ] I have read the [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/cognitx-leyton/.github/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) and agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/cognitx-leyton/.github/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
